briefs.title: BC Consumer Protection Rules Tighten: New Contract and Dispute Rights Take Effect August 2026
briefs.summary: Starting August 1, 2026, BC's updated consumer protection rules change how businesses must handle certain customer contracts and disputes. Key changes include: any purchase agreement over $50 (before tax) is now a 'future performance contract' (a deal where goods or services are delivered later), and businesses cannot use contract clauses to block customers from taking low-value claims (under $5,000) to court or a class action.
briefs.action_required: Review your customer contracts before August 1, 2026 to ensure they meet the new BC consumer protection rules.

key_dates.label: BC Consumer Protection Amendment Act 2025 — new contract and dispute rules take effect
